name: Dimmer Switch
conditioned_path: the 2am doomscroll where the screen is the only light left on in the room
mechanic: Press and hold anywhere; the screen brightness decays the longer you hold, lifting toward black. Release and it creeps back up. The whole game is holding the dimmer down through one long exhale until the screen goes fully dark — that black frame is the end.
video_trick: the feed that ends — the screen itself is the object being put down

name: Counting Sheep
conditioned_path: the autoplay loop you can't close, replaced by the oldest wind-down ritual
mechanic: Sheep drift one at a time over a fence; tap each as it leaps to count it. Every counted sheep slows the next one's drift and darkens the sky a shade. After the last sheep the field is black and the session closes — a loop with a built-in finish line.
video_trick: calm completion — the ritual that actually ends

name: Feed Fadeout
conditioned_path: the infinite autoplay feed engineered to outlast your willpower
mechanic: A vertical feed auto-scrolls on its own — you do nothing. As it plays, the scroll tempo slows, the audio fades, and the frames dim. You watch the autoplay mechanism run out of fuel until it stalls on a black frame. The game is letting the feed die instead of feeding it.
video_trick: the feed that ends — autoplay surrendered to stillness

name: Tempo Tether
conditioned_path: the rapid-cut hype edits that clock your resting heart rate up
mechanic: A soft orb pulses on a beat; tap as it peaks. Each clean tap lengthens the interval to the next pulse and dims the screen a notch. You are literally slowing your own tempo — by the end the beat is once every few seconds and the screen is black.
video_trick: calm completion — you slow the beat yourself

name: Scroll to Still
conditioned_path: the infinite-swipe thumb that keeps moving past every stopping point
mechanic: Swipe up slowly; the feed moves but each swipe adds friction and dims the screen a shade. The more you scroll, the heavier it gets, until the feed won't move at all and settles into black. The scroll habit is braked to a full stop instead of rewarded for speed.
video_trick: the feed that ends — friction as the off-ramp

name: Breath Dim
conditioned_path: late-night arousal held in the chest, with no pacing cue to bring it down
mechanic: Hold to inhale (screen brightens a touch), release to exhale (screen dims). Each full breath lowers the brightness baseline a step, so the resting level itself decays toward black across the session. Your breath is the dimmer, and sleep-onset is the decaying baseline.
video_trick: calm completion — biofeedback dimming to sleep

name: Lights Out
conditioned_path: the lit-room / blue-light exposure that keeps the brain reading 'daytime'
mechanic: A grid of soft glowing lights fills the screen; tap each to switch it off. Every light extinguished dims the whole screen and lowers a background hum. Turn them all off and the room is dark — the last light off is the session end.
video_trick: calm completion — darken the room one light at a time

name: Last Tab
conditioned_path: the 2am tab-hoarding rabbit hole — one more article, one more wiki
mechanic: Browser-style tabs stack on screen; swipe each one gently upward to close it. Each closed tab dims the screen and quiets the ambient sound. Close them all and the browser is empty and black — the open-loop tab hoard resolved to zero.
video_trick: calm completion — close every loop you opened

name: Heartbeat Slow
conditioned_path: the elevated heart rate the feed leaves you with at 2am
mechanic: A heartbeat thumps on screen; tap in time with each beat. Every matched tap drops the heart rate a few bpm and dims the screen. The goal is to walk the beat all the way down to a resting sleep rate and a black screen — slowing the body, not the feed.
video_trick: calm completion — bring the rate down yourself

name: Snowfall Settle
conditioned_path: the sensory loop that won't stop stimulating when you need stillness
mechanic: Snowflakes drift down; tap each one to gently settle it to rest. Each settled flake stops moving, darkens the sky a shade, and quiets the wind. When every flake is at rest and the sky is black, the storm has passed and the session ends.
video_trick: calm completion — settle the scene to still

name: Volume Fade
conditioned_path: autoplay audio — the sound that keeps the room from going quiet
mechanic: A low tone plays; hold to slowly lower the volume and dim the screen together. Release early and both creep back up. Hold through to silence and black and the session closes. You fade the stimulus out instead of letting it autoplay into the night.
video_trick: calm completion — fade the sound to nothing

name: Long Yawn
conditioned_path: the contagious-yawn reflex the body uses to signal sleep-onset
mechanic: A yawn ripple begins; hold to stretch it out longer. Each held yawn dims the screen a notch and slows the next ripple. Three full yawns and the screen is black — hijacking the body's own sleep-contagion cue as the mechanic.
video_trick: calm completion — the contagious yawn as the cue

name: Clock Melt
conditioned_path: the 2am bedside-clock-watching that makes time stretch instead of pass
mechanic: A bedside clock face shows; hold to slow the minute hand. The longer you hold, the more the hand drags and the screen dims, stretching time toward stillness. When the hand stops and the face goes black, time has finally let go and the session ends.
video_trick: shock-honesty — the clock you've been staring at, defused

name: Pixel by Pixel
conditioned_path: the lit screen as a field of wakefulness you can't switch off wholesale
mechanic: The screen is a dense field of lit pixels; each tap extinguishes a small cluster. The screen progressively darkens as you clear clusters, the hum quieting with each. Turn off the last lit pixel and the screen is fully black — sleep as pixel-by-pixel surrender.
video_trick: calm completion — darken the screen one tap at a time

name: Dimming Window
conditioned_path: blue-light / day-spectrum exposure that tells the brain it is still daytime
mechanic: A window looks out on a sky; hold to advance the sun from day toward night. The sky darkens, the color temperature warms down, and ambient daytime sound fades to silence. When the sky reaches full night and black, the body has its cue and the session closes.
video_trick: calm completion — run the sunset yourself

name: Slow the Loop
conditioned_path: seamless video loops that play forever with no exit cue
mechanic: A short looping animation plays; tap once per loop to lengthen its period. Each tap stretches the loop longer and dims the screen, until the loop period is many seconds long and the screen is black. You break the seamless loop by making it too slow to hold you.
video_trick: the feed that ends — the loop you slow until it stops

name: Candle Out
conditioned_path: the single light left on as a security against sleep
mechanic: A candle flame flickers; hold to let it burn lower. Each held moment shrinks the flame, dims the screen, and lowers the soft crackle. When the flame gutters out the screen is black — the last light you kept on, finally allowed to go.
video_trick: calm completion — let the last light go out

name: One More Stop
conditioned_path: the FYP 'one more video' autoplay lure that keeps you scrolling past every exit
mechanic: Cards autoplay like a FYP, each tagged 'one more?'. The only move is to NOT swipe — every second you hold still, the screen dims a shade; one swipe and it brightens back. Score is how many seconds you hold the dimmer down to black. The win is resisting the swipe that the algorithm lives on.
video_trick: loss aversion inverted — the score is how early you stop

name: Racing Mind
conditioned_path: the 2am thought-loop firing faster than you can settle it
mechanic: Thought bubbles fire across the screen fast; tap each to set it down before it escapes. Every caught thought slows the fire rate and dims the screen, until the thoughts barely come and the screen is black. Score is thoughts set down — the racing mind caught and slowed to sleep.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — catch every thought, then they stop

name: Autoplay Surrender
conditioned_path: the autoplay countdown bar that dares you to let the next video load
mechanic: A countdown bar sweeps toward the next 'video' like the autoplay timer. The move is to NOT tap stop — let each countdown run out, and each one dims the screen. Score is how many autoplays you surrender before the screen goes black. The discipline is letting the countdown win so you can lose the feed.
video_trick: anticipation agony — endure the countdown instead of dodging it
